criteria.description: Generate a docs retrieval index (title + heading-anchor slugs + short abstract, per page) over the page-inventory metadata spine.
criteria.evidence: WP01 approved (opus/reviewer-renata). scripts/docs/docs_index.py generate_index() + packaged src/specify_cli/docs/index_model.py render_index/parse_index; committed docs/development/3-2-docs-retrieval-index.yaml generated over the live 646-page docs/ tree. 24 tests in tests/docs/test_docs_index.py green.

criteria.description: spec-kitty docs query "<term>" [--json] command searching the index across title/anchors/abstract.
criteria.evidence: WP03 approved. src/specify_cli/cli/commands/docs.py Typer sub-app registered next to glossary; live smoke `spec-kitty docs query "worktree" --json` returns real matching pages. 14 tests in tests/docs/test_docs_query_cli.py green.
criteria.notes: Mirrors the glossary CLI shape; imports only the packaged specify_cli.docs.index_model.

criteria.description: Structured JSON result shape: path, title, matching anchors, abstract, divio_type; only the MATCHED anchors returned.
criteria.evidence: WP03. _matching_anchors() returns only anchors that hit the term/--section. Discriminating test: a >=2-anchor fixture where the term matches exactly one asserts the result contains ONLY that anchor; title-only match -> empty anchors. print(json.dumps(...)) JSON path (not Rich).
criteria.notes: Renata's non-fakeable FR-003 fixture requirement satisfied.

criteria.description: --divio-type and --section filters narrow to a Divio class / to pages containing a given heading anchor.
criteria.evidence: WP03. --divio-type validated against the correct value set (tutorial|how-to|reference|explanation|none, provably matching the committed index); --section filters to has-anchor-with-that-slug. Invalid --divio-type -> exit 2 no traceback. Tests green.
criteria.notes: DivioType validated via literal tuple (correct values); canonical-enum consolidation deferred as a documented follow-up.

criteria.description: CI-runnable freshness gate that regenerates the index and reports drift, mirroring the page-inventory lockfile gate.
criteria.evidence: WP02 approved. _check_docs_index_drift added to scripts/docs/check_docs_freshness.py as additive sub-check 6 (error-severity DOCS-INDEX-DRIFT); real mutate->detect->regenerate test asserts severity==error and a red aggregate exit; covered by docs-freshness.yml via the aggregate --ci entrypoint (T009 no-op). 7 tests green.

criteria.description: Deterministic, byte-stable index (alphabetical by path; deterministic anchor slugs).
criteria.evidence: WP01. Non-fakeable test: fixture files created in non-alphabetical order + direct ascending-path assertion; byte-stability verified across a real subprocess run with a different PYTHONHASHSEED. Live 646-row index round-trips under --strict (exit 0).

criteria.description: Full-tree docs query returns < 1s; no per-query filesystem walk (index loaded once).
criteria.evidence: WP03. Structural guard: a test spies Path.rglob and Path.open and asserts zero calls during store.query() after DocsIndexStore.load(); a wall-clock <1s live-tree smoke kept as a soft ceiling.
criteria.notes: Structural invariant, not just a machine-dependent timing smoke.

criteria.description: ruff + mypy clean on touched modules; complexity <=15; new branches/helpers covered by focused tests; no new suppressions.
criteria.evidence: All three WPs: uv run ruff check -> All checks passed; uv run mypy clean on the owned src/scripts modules (the pre-existing 'found twice under different module names' quirk reproduces on untouched files and is not introduced here); every function <=15 (ruff C901). 45 new focused tests across the three WPs.
criteria.notes: No new noqa/type:ignore introduced by the mission.
